tools.name: rancher_find_failing_pods
tools.description:
Find failing pods (CrashLoopBackOff, Pending, Failed, ImagePull errors).

    Omit `namespace` to triage the entire cluster in one call; pass it to
    scope to a single namespace.

tools.name: rancher_find_pdbs_blocking
tools.description:
Find PDBs blocking maintenance (currently allowing zero disruptions).

    Omit `namespace` to scan the whole cluster; pass it to scope to one.

tools.name: rancher_find_services_without_endpoints
tools.description:
Find services with no backing endpoints -- a fast outage signal.

    Omit `namespace` to scan the whole cluster; pass it to scope to one.

tools.name: rancher_find_stalled_rollouts
tools.description:
Find stalled rollouts (deployments/statefulsets not converging).

    Omit `namespace` to scan the whole cluster; pass it to scope to one.

tools.name: rancher_find_unbound_pvcs
tools.description:
Find unbound PVCs (Pending/Lost) — storage blocking app startup.

    Omit `namespace` to scan the whole cluster; pass it to scope to one.

tools.name: rancher_find_unready_nodes
tools.description: Find unready or unschedulable nodes across clusters.

tools.name: rancher_pod_logs
tools.description:
Fetch a pod container's recent log tail — the missing `kubectl logs`
    verb. Omit `container` for a single-container pod; a multi-container pod
    without one returns a clean error listing the available container names
    so you can retry. Set `previous=true` to read the last terminated
    instance's logs (diagnosing a crash loop) instead of the current one.

tools.name: rancher_resource_events
tools.description:
List recent Kubernetes events for one named resource (Pod, Deployment,
    PersistentVolumeClaim, ...) — "what just happened to this thing",
    generalized from `pod_get`'s inlined pod-scoped events to any `kind`.
    Most recent first, capped to the last ~20.
